subject: GRB 220513A: Fermi GBM Final Real-time Localization
body:
The Fermi GBM team reports the detection of a likely SHORT GRB

At 20:46:08 UT on 13 May 2022, the Fermi Gamma-ray Burst Monitor (GBM) triggered and located GRB 220513A (trigger 674167573.559368 / 220513865).

The on-ground calculated location, using the Fermi GBM trigger data, is RA = 83.9, Dec = -29.2 (J2000 degrees, equivalent to J2000 05h 35m, -29d 11'), with a statistical uncertainty of 12.1 degrees.

The angle from the Fermi LAT boresight is 97.0 degrees.

The skymap can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2022/bn220513865/quicklook/glg_skymap_all_bn220513865.png

The HEALPix FITS file, including the estimated localization systematic, can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2022/bn220513865/quicklook/glg_healpix_all_bn220513865.fit

The GBM light curve can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2022/bn220513865/quicklook/glg_lc_medres34_bn220513865.gif
